text:
WHY $SOFI IS A TOP 3 POSITION FOR ME

The market is overpricing the credit fear and underpricing the platform optionality when it comes to SoFi which is why I think it's one of the weirdest setups in the market right now. 

The business is still executing very well but the stock is stuck in one of the worst sentiment buckets since the market clearly doesn't want exposure to credit-sensitive stocks in a higher-for-longer rate environment and SoFi still gets treated like a bank-like lender even though the business is becoming much broader than that.

The Loan Platform Business (LPB) is more important than people realize since SoFi originated $3B of personal loans on behalf of third parties through LPB in the quarter and added another $3.6B of capital commitments from three new partners. That matters because it gives SoFi flexibility so they can choose which loans to keep on the balance sheet and which loans to push through partners for fee revenue without taking the same balance sheet risk (very different model than being a traditional lender that is stuck holding everything). 

This is also why the private credit fear looked less scary if you listened to the actual earnings call. One of the biggest bear arguments was that funding partners would pull back if credit conditions weakened but Noto said they're not seeing issues in performance or partner demand and LPB demand was actually above what they chose to fulfill.

The member flywheel is still the main reason this is a top 3 position for me because this is a real moat the market is overlooking. SoFi added over 1M members in the quarter, ~45% of new products came from existing members and 50% of SoFi Plus sign-ups took another product. The more members they add, the more products they cross-sell, the more deposits they gather, the more revenue they generate per member and the more efficient the platform becomes (how SoFi becomes more valuable over time). SoFi Plus becoming a paid subscription is also great to see because recurring fee revenue on top of a growing user base can improve unit economics over time. 

The biggest thing I want to see next is the Tech Platform turning around which is obviously the weakest link. Technology Platform revenue fell 27% YoY to $75M, contribution profit fell 61% and total accounts declined because of the large client that transitioned off the platform. Management is rebranding it as SoFi Tech Solutions and breaking it into processing, banking core ledgers, payment hub, and risk and fraud. If that platform starts growing again then great but I view this more as a free call option on owning SoFi.

I truly believe SoFi is a good business trapped in a bad sentiment bucket. The market is focused on rates, credit risk and the weak Technology Platform while I'm focused on members, deposits, cross-buy, loan platform flexibility, operating leverage and the long-term path toward becoming a top financial institution.

It can absolutely trade lower if the market sells off or if credit fears come back but I think the business is much stronger than the stock action suggests and I have no problem keeping this a top 3 position for me.

$IONQ is a top 5 position in my growth portfolio but context really matters because I built a 11% position when the stock at ~$6. 

Quantum is still very early and I don’t think anyone should pretend it is going to have its “ChatGPT moment” anytime soon since large-scale commercial quantum advantage is still years away and timeline could easily take longer than the market wants.

That said I’m willing to underwrite the volatility because I view quantum more as a national security theme because the first major customers will likely be governments, defense agencies, national labs, pharma companies & energy companies due to the implications for encryption, navigation, timing, sensing & secure communications.

That said, I have a lot of cushion here, and I’m still willing to underwrite the volatility because I think the first major quantum customers will be governments, defense agencies, national labs, pharma companies and energy companies.

If quantum becomes important then the category should be big enough for specialized leaders to matter and I think IonQ has a real shot at being the pure-play category leader.

All portfolio performance and transaction data has been independently validated by a third party:

SpaceX is expected to IPO next month which could force the market to start treating space economy as foundational infrastructure with durable demand from both government & commercial customers.

That could lift entire public space ecosystem across launch, LEO connectivity & orbital infrastructure.

A bullish setup for me is when the 9-day EMA is above the 21-day EMA & the stock is holding both the 50-day & 200-day SMA so both momentum + structure are moving in the same direction.

If the only level a stock is holding is the 200-day SMA then the long-term trend is still intact but the momentum has faded so I treat it as a hold.

When the price breaks below all three levels then that is when I consider the trend bearish because the short-term, intermediate & long-term signals are all pointing the same way.

KEY LEVELS TO WATCH FOR POPULAR STOCKS

• Bullish: $NVDA, $TSLA, $AMD, $MU, $AVGO, $NBIS, $ONDS, $AEHR, $CIFR, $RKLB, $IONQ, $IREN, $AAOI
• Hold: $HOOD, $OKLO, $ASTS, $HIMS
• Bearish: $PLTR, $OPEN, $SOFI

Project Thor refers to a conceptual kinetic orbital bombardment weapons system. It involves dropping large, inert tungsten rods (often described as telephone pole-sized: ~20 feet long, 1 foot in diameter) from satellites in orbit to strike ground targets at hypersonic speeds

In today's  Early Look: "The Future of Wall Street Is Already Here"

Bohm wrote that in 1980. He wasn't talking about AI. He was talking about the nature of reality itself — that nothing is a fixed object, everything is a process becoming something else.

Forty-five years later, I spent three days at HedgeyeLIVE watching that sentence come to life in real time.

The future of Wall Street isn't coming. It's already here. 

You can see it in how our power users are working, how signals are being processed, how decisions are being made.
Don't worry, I'm awake and grinding - just no time to tweet (yet) this morn with our HedgeyeLIVE conference in its final day

Here’s my brief note (a part of it)

Japan is the global liquidity.

That’s what I’ve been screaming for over 2.5 years.

What’s going on in the bond market, and specifically when it relates to Japan’s yields, should scare the mother fucking piss out of you, your friend’s girlfriend’s dog walker’s ex-husband’s barber, your hamster, and ESPECIALLY your mother’s lover’s mistress five times removed.

So I have been saying this and literally writing about it for years. 

It does not mean I am a bear. But Risk Aware. This is the reality of investing.

The robots have clocked in.

@JAL_Official_jp is deploying humanoids at Haneda Airport as part of a three-year operational commitment.

@BMW validated humanoid robots through over 1,250 operational hours on an active assembly line and is scaling to Europe.

$AMZN is building a consumer physical AI platform.

@UnitreeRobotics is targeting an IPO valuation of USD $3-7 billion.

This is no longer a science project.

The supply chain question is where the real investment thesis is being built, and the EV analogy from 2012 is instructive: the most durable value will likely accrue to the component categories with the highest technical barriers, actuators, force sensors, and precision reducers, not the headline names.

$KOID holds 50 companies across the broad humanoid and physical AI ecosystem, equal-weighted and globally diversified across the US, China, Japan, and Europe.

Similar to what $NVDA and semiconductors did for digital AI, these are the picks and shovels for physical AI.

Read our full 2026 humanoid robotics outlook: bit.ly/4woJw7X

KOID Top 10 Holdings: rebrand.ly/qu1gxvj

Why explain the future of robotics… when a humanoid robot can do it for you?

At our @NasdaqExchange  event, @intbotai  broke down how KOID gives investors exposure to the humanoid robotics revolution and what that future looks like in real time.
